verse_specific_anchors: The PIVOT character — explicit reference to 'this is the verse that closes the karma-yoga argument and pivots the chapter' and 'sets up Arjuna's question at 2.54 (how does such a person speak, sit, walk?)' — is verse-specific to 2.53. 2.52 also concerns buddhi-yoga maturation but does not pivot to sthita-prajña.

The LAYERED niścalā + acalā distinction is verse-specific. 2.52 names the thicket-crossing (single transition); 2.53 names two qualifications of the resulting buddhi (niścalā = unmoving with respect to external, acalā = unwavering once unmoving). The engineering body explicitly distinguishes 'not pulled by external claims' from 'not internally re-litigating once the pulls have been resisted'.

The closing condition framing — tadā yogam avāpsyasi (THEN you will attain yoga) — is grammatically forward-looking and verse-specific. The engineering analog's '20-verses-onwards-foreshadowing' framing is anchored on this grammatical move.
verdict_reason: Some overlap with 2.52 (both concern buddhi-yoga maturation). The analog's specific anchors — pivot character, layered niścalā+acalā distinction, closing-condition + foreshadowing — are 2.53's contributions. An informed reader given the analog and asked which of 2.49-2.53 it adapts would converge on 2.53 because of the explicit 2.54 pivot reference. Pass.
